title: Complex noun phrases
explanation:
Complex noun phrases

C1 writers pack information into noun phrases using pre-modifiers and post-modifiers.

  • the recently published government report on climate policy
  • a highly controversial decision taken by the board
  • the extent to which regulations are enforced

questions.text: Choose the most natural phrase: [*the government's recently published climate report|the climate report government published recent|recent climate government report published].
[*The extent to which|The extent which|The extent in which] regulations are enforced remains unclear.
A [*widely accepted|accepted widely|accepting wide] theory explains the phenomenon.
[*A decision taken by the board|A taken decision by board|Decision by board taken] surprised investors.
The [*analysis of long-term economic trends|analysis long-term of trends economic|long-term trends analysis economic of] informed the policy.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: We need a more detailed ____ of the risks involved. | *assessment | assess | assessing
The committee discussed the ____ to which funding should increase. | *extent | extend | extensive
This is a highly ____ proposal with serious implications. | *contentious | content | contently
The report contains an ____ of recent migration patterns. | *overview | oversee | overseen
[*The factors influencing public trust|The factors public trust influencing|Influencing factors public trust] are complex.
[*A strategy designed to reduce waste|A designed strategy to waste reduce|Strategy reduce designed waste] was adopted.
[*The degree of public support|The degree public support of|The public degree support of] surprised analysts.
[*An approach favoured by many experts|An favoured approach by experts many|Approach by favoured experts] gained attention.
[*The implications of the ruling|The implications the ruling of|Of implications the ruling] are still debated.
